_spec: BANZA QR Code Lifecycle Specification v1.0

states.ACTIVE: QR code is valid and accepts payment.
states.USED: QR code was successfully paid. No further payments accepted. Terminal state.
states.EXPIRED: QR code TTL has elapsed. No further payments accepted. Terminal state.

STATIC.description: Reusable QR code. Linked to an owner identity (consumer or merchant). Amount is set by the payer at scan time.

transitions.note: Static QR does NOT transition to USED after payment. It remains ACTIVE for future use.

DYNAMIC.description: Single-use QR code. Amount is pre-set by the creator. Expires after TTL or after one successful payment.

transitions.note: Transition is atomic with the ledger posting. If the posting fails, status does not change.

transitions.trigger: expires_at <= now (checked by expiry worker or at payment time)
transitions.note: Expiry is enforced both proactively by the expiry worker and reactively at payment time. A QR that reaches EXPIRED cannot be paid even if the expiry worker has not run yet.

invariants: INV-QR-001: MUST be paid at most once
INV-QR-002: expires_at MUST be set (not null)

payment_flow.description: The canonical QR payment sequence from scan to ledger posting.

steps.action: Consumer device parses prefix (BANZAMI: or BANZAMI-SBX:) and decodes Base64url body
Type discriminator 't' determines STATIC or DYNAMIC path
STATIC path: resolve owner_id from payload; consumer confirms amount
DYNAMIC path: extract QR code ID, call operator to fetch record, verify HMAC signature, verify ACTIVE status and non-expired TTL
Consumer confirms payment on their device
Operator executes atomic ledger posting: debit consumer wallet, credit merchant wallet
DYNAMIC only: mark QR code as USED (atomic with ledger posting)
Emit qr.paid event with trace_id, qr_id, transfer_id, amount_minor
Return success response with transfer_id and paid_at

payment_flow.atomicity: Steps 6 and 7 (ledger posting + QR status update) MUST be atomic. It is not acceptable to post the ledger entry but leave the QR in ACTIVE status, or to mark USED without the ledger entry. INV-LEDGER-004.

QR_NOT_FOUND.description: No QR code record exists for the given ID.

QR_ALREADY_USED.description: Dynamic QR code has already been paid. INV-QR-001.

AMOUNT_REQUIRED.description: Open-amount QR was presented with no amount_minor in the pay request.

INVALID_SIGNATURE.description: HMAC verification of dynamic QR payload failed.

INVALID_PAYLOAD.description: QR payload string cannot be decoded or parsed.

expiry_worker.description: Operators SHOULD run a background expiry worker that periodically transitions ACTIVE dynamic QR codes to EXPIRED when expires_at <= now. The reference implementation uses an async Tokio task on a 30-second interval.
expiry_worker.note: The expiry worker is an optimisation. The payment path MUST also check expiry synchronously at payment time, regardless of whether the expiry worker has run.

invariants.INV-QR-001: A dynamic QR code MUST be paid at most once. Second payment attempt on a USED QR MUST return HTTP 422.
invariants.INV-QR-002: Static QR: expires_at MUST be null. Dynamic QR: expires_at MUST be set and in the future at creation time.
invariants.INV-QR-003: A QR code with status EXPIRED or USED MUST reject payment.
invariants.INV-QR-004: Ledger posting and QR status update to USED MUST be atomic. Partial state (ledger without status update, or status update without ledger) is a financial invariant violation.
invariants.INV-QR-SIGN-001: Dynamic QR HMAC signature MUST be verified against the database record before payment. Payload-only verification is insufficient.
